cells.discriminating_fact: In-cell (compute.py, exact/symbolic): (1) gamma5 GENUINELY DERIVES — the incidence matrix M=[[1,1],[1,0]] has char poly lambda^2-lambda-1, discriminant EXACTLY 5, so its splitting field is Q(sqrt5) with Gal=Z/2 sending phi<->1-phi; eig(M)={1-phi, phi} and phi*(1-phi)=det(M)=-1. This is the SAME field, SAME numbers, SAME involution as B766's gamma5 ((1-phi)^2=phi^-2). Forced, reproduced two ways, not base-rate (sigma's growth field IS Q(sqrt5)). (2) c does NOT derive — the letter-swap s:a<->b is NOT an automorphism of sigma: s.sigma.s = {a->b, b->ba} != sigma (sigma is letter-asymmetric: a spawns 2, b spawns 1). det(M)=-1 is an orientation VALUE, not a free involution, and encodes no self/other side. The case bit {a,A} the courier assigns to c is not in sigma's alphabet {a,b} — imported. (3) theta does NOT derive as identified — a native Z/2 exists (subshift reversal sigma~={a->ba,b->a}; subdominant eig(T)=-1/phi<0) but its map to the object's MATRIX-level SL(2)-trace theta is unforced: the -1/phi route is E20-UNLINKED (quarantined to cell P2-WELD) and the reversal route lacks a substitution<->trace-map bridge. (4) Counting obstruction: sigma's 2-letter alphabet carries 1 native letter-bit + the eigenvalue-Galois bit; it cannot canonically source THREE independent named involutions. The courier's 4-letter {a,A,b,B} carries only 2 independent bits, so its 3 distinctions {case,position,pair} are dependent (one = XOR of the other two) — matching B766 chord=c XOR theta. The object's rank-3 discrete menu lives on the richer character-variety/CM structure (B766), not on the thin substitution.

cells.headline: The D5=Spin(10) McKay analogy fails as an object connection: the generic McKay(BD3)=affine D5=Spin(10) identity is true but B727-forced (P=1), while the object's native McKay ancestor 2T (order 24, to affine E6) does not contain the D5 ancestor BD3 (order 12) and emits E6, not D5; Spin(10) touches the object only as a Lie subgroup of E6 (27=16+10+1), a non-McKay embedding already walled (B570 fifth wall, vector-like).
cells.discriminating_fact: In-cell computed: (A) the McKay graph of the binary-dihedral/dicyclic group Dic3 (order 12), built from explicit SU(2) matrices of all 6 irreps (relations + character orthonormality self-checked), equals affine D5^(1): degree seq {1,1,1,1,3,3}, Perron eigenvalue 2, marks {1,1,1,1,2,2} sum=8=h(D5), matching spectrum. (B) D5 Lie = so(10) = Lie(Spin(10)) (2n=10, dim 45, roots+rank=45, Cartan det 4). BUT (C) the object's only native McKay ancestor is 2T=SL(2,3) (order 24) to affine E6; two independent proofs show BD3 (order 12) is NOT a subgroup of 2T: 2T^ab=Z/3 has no index-2 subgroup, and an exhaustive 2-generated-subgroup search yields orders {1,2,3,4,6,8,24} with no order-12 subgroup. So the object emits E6, never D5. Spin(10) reaches the object ONLY as the Lie subgroup so(10) in e6 (27=16+10+1; 78=45+1+16+16bar verified), a Lie-subalgebra branching, not the McKay finite-subgroup correspondence, and its chiral 16 is the walled B570 fifth wall (theta-stable principal SL(2,C) holonomy, 27 self-dual/vector-like). The generic McKay-Lie D5 to Spin(10) identity is real but forced for every ADE label (B727 P=1), hence not object-specific evidence.

VERIFIED IN-CELL & INDEPENDENTLY:
- Part A (McKay(Dic3,order12)=affine D5): the code builds all 6 irreps from explicit SU(2) matrices, self-checks group relations (r^6=1, s^2=r^3, srs^-1=r^-1) and character orthonormality (=> genuinely irreducible+complete, sum dim^2=12). - Part B (D5=so(10)=Lie(Spin10)): dim 45, roots 40, rank 5, 40+5=45, 2n=10, Cartan det=4. Correct.
- Part C (the discriminating fact, load-bearing): the object's native McKay ancestor is 2T=SL(2,3)→affine E6; BD3(order12) is NOT ⊂ 2T, proven TWO independent ways in-cell: (i) 2T^ab=Z/3 odd ⇒ no index-2 subgroup; (ii) exhaustive 2-generated-subgroup closure gives orders {1,2,3,4,6,8,24}, no 12. I re-derived both independently (|derived|=8, ab-order=3, subgroup orders {1,2,3,4,6,8,24}, no order-12) — confirmed. The "≤2-generated" premise is valid (all 5 groups of order 12 are ≤2-generated). This is the classic SL(2,3)-has-no-index-2-subgroup fact, computed not cited. So the object emits E6, never D5. Genuine object-nativity refutation.

NOT A FORCED POSITIVE: the cell correctly flags the generic McKay(BD3)=affine D5=Spin(10) identity as TRUE-but-B727-forced (P=1 for every ADE label), and REFUSES to read that generic truth as RESOLVED-A. cells.headline: S032-A no-forced-choice: theta-odd sector WALLED at zero, algebraic sector fully symmetrizable, all-invariant theorem reduced to one specialist (arithmetic-of-volumes) residual -- no counterexample; not forced to a positive.
cells.discriminating_fact: A structural TRICHOTOMY exhausts every natural invariant of the single-seed metallic object, and no branch supplies conditions (2)&(3) [discretely-multivalued AND unsymmetrizable]. (B) theta-ODD sector -- the B774-flagged place a counterexample would hide -- is WALLED at ZERO by EXACT amphichirality: R^m L^m has mirror = conjugation (L^m R^m = R^{-m}(R^m L^m)R^m holds exactly in SL(2,Z), verified m=1..6), and the fig-8 self-mirror shape z-bar = 1-z is exact, forcing CS = -CS = 0 while the theta-even volume 2.02988 stays alive (structural zero, not even=odd cancellation). (A) EVERY discretely-multivalued ALGEBRAIC invariant is SYMMETRIZABLE: monodromy=Galois makes the value multiset G-stable, so elementary symmetric functions lie in the fixed field -- INVARIANT-AGNOSTIC, verified on golden {phi,phi'} (sum 1, prod -1), Eisenstein CP {e^{+-i pi/6}} (sum sqrt3, prod 1), a genuine theta-odd toy {+t,-t} (e1=0, e2=-t^2), and a MIXED canonical-plus-Galois-pair set (all symmetric fns canonical) -- covering trace-ring, torsion, quantum/WRT, Ptolemy, Bloch class, and SL(n>=3) Falbel components. (C) theta-EVEN transcendental invariants: the canonical case, hyperbolic volume, VARIES continuously on the free-kappa fixed locus (samples 1.0104/1.0149/1.0108), failing discreteness. NO counterexample exists (gate survives); a single-seed forced choice would require HETEROGENEITY (>=2 distinct seeds, B131 -- the (1,2) fork kappa in {-4,-2} is itself rational & trace-indexed, hence symmetrizable).
cells.notes: Verdict UNRESOLVED / NEEDS-SPECIALIST, honoring the B772 anti-unearned-positive discipline: I did NOT upgrade B330's explicit "conditional/open" to a clean RESOLVED-A theorem. Two of three branches are sealed unconditionally in-cell (theta-odd EXACT-zero; all-algebraic symmetrizable via monodromy=Galois, reproduced two ways where positive). The genuine residual is branch (C): a fully general proof that no NATURAL theta-even transcendental invariant is discretely-multivalued for EVERY cover / SL(n) is the arithmetic-of-volumes (Borel regulator / scissors-congruence) corner that B330 named -- that is the EXTERNAL/specialist target. This is a strong partial WALL and a real advance beyond B330: it closes the Bloch/Ptolemy/SL(n>=3)-Falbel "untested classes" (now inside branch A) and reduces the open target from "all invariant classes" to the single transcendental-even/component-orbit corner, which is exactly the B131 heterogeneity threshold. Per B775 house method a WALLED sub-result + a genuine reduction + a named EXTERNAL residual is a SUCCESS, not a failure. Cell: /Users/dri/origin-axiom/frontier/B775_phase2_wave1/cells/P2-NOFORCE/ (compute.py, output.txt, results.json). Gate 5-Q: structural language only, no SM values, no consciousness claims, nothing to CLAIMS.md, one-number pin untouched. compute.py is re-runnable (pyenv python3 + sympy 1.14).
cells.verify_note:
Rerun of compute.py reproduces output.txt byte-for-byte and results.json (modulo elapsed_seconds). Verdict UNRESOLVED / NEEDS-SPECIALIST is the honest conservative terminal state and matches the criterion (specialist residual => UNRESOLVED); no forced positive (the B772 risk is absent — the cell explicitly does NOT claim a full theorem, leaving branch C as the named arithmetic-of-volumes residual). Branch A (algebraic => G-stable multiset => symmetric functions in fixed field => symmetrizable) is a standard, correctly invariant-agnostic lemma with valid toy confirmations. Branch B logic (f=-f=0 from conjugacy-invariance + theta-odd sign flip) is valid, and the theta-odd test object (Chern-Simons) is genuinely non-trace-polynomial (passes the B774/W3-082c self-test, not a relabeled trace invariant). Branch C is honestly left open with the canonical case (volume) shown continuous. Gate clean: structural language only, no SM values, no consciousness claims.

Non-flipping caveats worth recording: (1) Branch B's per-m "amphichirality exact for m=1..6" is the UNIVERSAL AB~BA fact (L^m R^m = R^{-m}(R^m L^m)R^m is a tautology for any matrices) — cosmetic window-dressing, not a discriminating computation. (2) cs_theta_odd=0.0 is hardcoded ("forced by self-mirror"), a genuine known fig-8 fact but asserted rather than derived in-cell. (3) counterexample_found is hardcoded False (no computational search), so the verdict logic structurally cannot emit RESOLVED-B and never emits RESOLVED-A. None of these inflate the verdict: the final state is the humble UNRESOLVED, they are consistent with genuine facts, and branches A/B are standard-sound while C is honestly deferred. Upheld.

cells.headline: Row-16 silence + ±1/48 sign-selection PROVEN as a theorem: row 16 is the UNIQUE silent-but-supported row (arithmetic ℚ(√5)-reality, mirror-reduced to row-evenness), and the ±1/48 seam sign is the √5-Galois face — both reproduced two independent ways in exact Q(ζ60) arithmetic.
cells.discriminating_fact: In-cell, exact over Q(ζ60) (compute.py, all 240 cells): (1) SUPPORT rule — nonzero rows are EXACTLY K1={0,1,4,5,6,9,11,14,15,16,19}; the other 9 "silent" rows are entirely zero, so silence is non-trivial for row 16 alone. (2) UNIQUENESS — among the 11 supported rows, row 16 is the ONLY one with z=s=0 (ℚ(√5)-dark), carrying genuine real content t(16,0)=(1/24,1/120,0,0), t(16,4)=t(16,8)=(5/48,-1/240,0,0). (3) MIRROR law τ₃(t(a,b))=t(a,-b) holds 240/240 ⟹ silence(a)⟺row-evenness; row 16 even, its twin row 4 odd/bright. (4) NOT symmetry-forced: rows 4 and 16 obey identical covariances (mirror + R1 b→7b), yet an EXHAUSTIVE Galois scan finds NO σ_c mapping raw row-4→row-16 (raw row-16 fixed only by {1,31}=the ℚ(ζ15)-stabilizer) — the darkness is the arithmetic vanishing of the √-3/√-15 content of the level-15 theta values, proven finite-exact. (5) SIGN selection — the 8 pure-seam |s|=1/48 cells satisfy s=ε(a)·z with clean sector sign ε∈{+1 (a=6,14), -1 (a=0,4)}; σ_7 (the √5-flip) flips the seam s and fixes the √-3 part z (flagship t(0,4)=(-1/48,-1/80,-1/48,+1/48) → (-1/48,+1/80,-1/48,-1/48)); 1/48=(1/4)(1/12), 1/12=1/16+1/48; and the two sector constants are exact σ_√5 conjugates (0,0,-1/12,-1/12)↔(0,0,-1/12,+1/12) [φ↔-1/φ]. Hence the ±1/48 sign IS Galois-forced (the √5-face), while the silence is finite-exact arithmetic reality.
cells.notes: Cell dir: /Users/dri/origin-axiom/frontier/B775_phase2_wave1/cells/P2-SELRULE/ (compute.py re-runnable, output.txt, results.json). Builds on B379/B382/B383/B399 machinery (B358 cyclo_engine + B367 step0). B774 discipline: t(a,b)=tr(Par·P_a·Q_b) IS a symmetric trace/character invariant (correct object for a selection rule), NOT a claimed non-abelian chord — no violation. Honest scope: the census's literal ask ("derive the silence from Weyl/dihedral structure as a symmetry theorem") is only PARTIALLY met — the SIGN is genuinely Galois-forced (σ_√5), but the SILENCE is proven to be arithmetic-reality NOT single-symmetry-forced (V4); the mirror law reduces it to row-evenness, an equivalence, and finite-exact verification is a complete proof for this fixed level-15 object. This structural characterization of the silence's *nature* (arithmetic, not symmetry-imposed; row 16 unique among supported rows) is itself the earned "why." Gate 5/5-Q clean: structural language only, no SM values, no consciousness claims, nothing to CLAIMS, one-number pin untouched. Sharper than B383 (which did not establish the support=K1 framing nor the uniqueness among supported rows nor the no-forcing-symmetry finding).
cells.verify_note:
RERUN: compute.py reproduces output.txt BIT-IDENTICALLY (exit 0, empty diff). Fact is computed IN-CELL: the trace table C is BUILT FRESH from build_theta_W (raw DFT + diagonal Weil matrices, genuine twisted trace par_trace=tr(Par*A*B)=sum A[-x,y]B[y,x]), NOT loaded from the committed C_theta.json — a strong point.

INDEPENDENT VERIFICATION (fresh mpmath, dps 35-50, bypassing the project's exact cyclo_engine entirely) confirms every load-bearing fact: (1) support = K1={0,1,4,5,6,9,11,14,15,16,19} exactly; (2) row 16 is the UNIQUE silent-AND-supported row (Im(H_avg)=0 for all b); (3) t(16,0)=(1/24,1/120,0,0) exactly with genuine real content; (4) flagship t(0,4)=(-1/48,-1/80,-1/48,1/48) exactly; (5) σ_7 fixes {1,√-3} and flips {√5,√-15} — the √5-Galois face — confirmed via the v0±v7 component split. The H_avg = (1/2)(1+σ_4) on Q(ζ15) is the correct orthogonal projection onto H=Q(√5,√-3) (idempotent, σ_4 involution fixing H); solve_H asserts membership. Silence is a genuine property of the declared observable (√-15 ∈ H ⊂ Q(ζ15), so bright rows really do carry it and row 16 really does not) — not a projection artifact.

B774 LESSON RESPECTED (not violated): the cell EXPLICITLY declares (lines 15-17) that t(a,b)=tr(Par P_a Q_b) IS a symmetric trace invariant/character, NOT a claimed non-abelian chord, and correctly frames the result as a SELECTION RULE about trace invariants. No relabeling of a character sum as something exotic.

VERDICT LOGIC is genuinely three-valued: UNRESOLVED is reachable (e.g. mirror_all False while v1/v2/sector_clean/galois True ⇒ silence_proven False, rule_fails False ⇒ UNRESOLVED); RESOLVED-B reachable via rule_fails. The V4 "not-symmetry-forced" negative is an EXHAUSTIVE finite Galois scan (computed, not asserted): no σ_c maps raw row-4→row-16, raw row-16 stabilizer = {1,31} = Q(ζ15)-stabilizer. Gate 5/5-Q clean: structural language only, no SM values, no consciousness claims, nothing to CLAIMS.

"THEOREM" is defensible: for a finite structure (240 cells, exact arithmetic) exhaustive exact verification IS a proof; the uniqueness/support/mirror claims are exhaustive and not overclaimed as an infinite general theorem.

ONE FRAMING NUANCE (not a material failure): V5 "reproduction 2" (σ_7 flips seam s, fixes z) is AUTOMATIC for ANY H-element (σ_√5 always flips the √5/√-15 basis vectors), so it certifies WHICH automorphism is the √5-face rather than independently deriving the sign. The genuine sign-SELECTION content is reproduction 1 — the exhaustively-verified clean per-sector relation s=ε(a)·z with ε∈{±1} on the 8 pure-seam |s|=1/48 cells (ε=0 would falsify; it does not) — which is a real, exact, in-cell selection rule. The "±1/48 sign is Galois-forced" phrasing is a legitimate structural description (the two sectors ARE σ_√5-conjugate: (0,0,-1/12,-1/12)↔(0,0,-1/12,+1/12)), not a false computed fact. Insufficient to disturb the verdict.

RESOLVED-A upheld: the row-16 silence (unique, non-trivial real content, mirror-reduced to evenness, arithmetically not-symmetry-forced) and the ±1/48 seam sign-selection rule are both genuinely proven by exact exhaustive computation and independently reproduced.
cells.verify_issues: Minor framing only, not material: V5 repro-2 (σ_7 flips seam / fixes z) is automatic for any H-element, so it identifies the √5-Galois face rather than independently deriving the sign; the real sign-selection content is the exhaustively-verified clean per-sector s=ε(a)·z (repro-1). The "±1/48 is Galois-forced" language is a structural description (sectors are σ_√5-conjugate), not an overclaim of a false fact. No computed fact is wrong; nothing rises to a material failure.
